home / blog

PyCon PH 2024 as Chair

March 1, 2024
pythonph pyconph pyconph2024 community conference

Originally published on Medium/@pythonph. Reposted here with personal reflections added.

As PyCon PH 2024 wrapped up four days ago, we're filled with a mix of joy and nostalgia. Over those two days, our vibrant community came together in a celebration of Python, technology, and collaboration. We hope that every attendee, whether you were here to learn, network, make friends, seek job opportunities, recruit talent, or simply immerse yourself in the Python community, found what you were looking for and left with a little more than you came with.

Gratitude for Our Community

This event would not have been possible without the generous support of our sponsors and partners, the enthusiasm and participation of our attendees, and the tireless dedication of our volunteers. Your contributions shaped this conference into an unforgettable experience. Visit pycon-2024.python.ph/volunteers to meet the pillar behind PyCon PH 2024.

Captured Moments

For more snapshots of the conference, check out our collection of unofficial photos captured by other attendees:

  • Google Photos album
  • Photos by our media partner Devbits

Relive the memories and share your own using our official hashtags:

#gabay #pyconph2024 #pyconph #pythonph

Your Voice Matters

Your input is crucial in making PyCon PH even better. Please take a moment to fill out the Conference Feedback Form. Share your thoughts on what you loved and where we can improve.

Parting Thoughts

As we bid farewell to PyCon PH 2024, we hope it has provided you with valuable guidance (Gabay) on your chosen path, whether in professional development, networking, or personal growth within the Python community.

Even as PyCon PH 2024 concludes, remember that Python Philippines (PythonPH) remains a vibrant, supportive community. We're here to continue offering guidance, resources, and opportunities for growth, networking, and learning all year round.

Personal Reflection (2026)

Looking back at this post, there's one thing I didn't mention in the official write-up.

On Day 2 of the conference, I had to give the opening remarks. And I decided, for some reason, that it would be a good idea to sing.

The song was Celestial by Ed Sheeran. Our theme that year was Gabay (Guide), and the lyrics fit perfectly:

"You make me feel like my troubled heart is a million miles away"

That line resonated with me. The PythonPH community was exactly that for me — a place where the stress and trouble of daily life just... faded. So I stood up in front of everyone and sang it.

Was it good? Debatable. Was it embarrassing? Absolutely. But the room was kind, and honestly, it captured how I felt about the community better than any formal speech could have. Sometimes the cringey moments become the most memorable ones.

That's what PyCon PH was about to me. Not just the talks and the workshops, but the fact that you could stand in front of 200+ people, sing an Ed Sheeran song, and they'd cheer for you anyway.

That's the Gabay spirit.
